mission.title: dify: Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated Execution and Interval Control
mission.description: Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated Execution and Interval Control. Requested by 12+ users.

steps.title: Check current dify deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dify version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
helm list -n dify 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dify installation.

steps.title: Review dify configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dify configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get configmap -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dify
```
### Self Checks

- [X] I have searched for existing issues [search for existing issues](https://github.com/langgenius/dify/issues), including closed ones.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated
steps.description:
# Summary

This PR introduces the Loop Node, a feature designed to enable workflows or processes to execute repeatedly until specific conditions or formats are met. The node allows users to define internal variables and outputs that determine whether to exit the loop. A maximum iteration limit can also be set to prevent infinite loops. 

The addition of the Loop Node addresses the community’s and team’s demand for executing repeated HTTP interface calls or invoking LLMs multiple times in a

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dify -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dify
kubectl get events -n dify --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Enhancing Workflow with Loop and Sleep Nodes for Repeated" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# Summary

This PR introduces the Loop Node, a feature designed to enable workflows or processes to execute repeatedly until specific conditions or formats are met. The node allows users to define internal variables and outputs that determine whether to exit the loop. A maximum iteration limit can also be set to prevent infinite loops.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dify installed or the issue environment reproducible.
